rationale.R1.1: A section review centrally includes checking whether the contribution framing and gap claim are coherent at the conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer would be expected to assess whether the argument linking method, results, generalization, and conclusion is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes multiple empirical claims, so reviewing it requires checking whether those claims are aligned with the reported metrics and comparisons.
rationale.R1.5: Because the artifact is a full section, review reasonably includes assessing subsection-level ordering and narrative progression.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended by the short delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains novelty, generalization, and deployment-oriented claims, making overclaim avoidance a central responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation asks only for review of this section, so the projected responsibilities must remain limited to that artifact and task.
rationale.RX.4: Claims about calibration and high-stakes deployment could affect downstream interpretation, so harm from unsupported or overstated conclusions should be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: Any review responsibility should track which statements in the section motivate the projected concerns.
